title: On the categorical local Langlands conjectures for depth-zero regular supercuspidal representations

thm:2.1.10.summary: For a tame regular semisimple elliptic $L$-parameter (TRSELP) $\varphi$, the connected component $X_\varphi$ of the stack of $L$-parameters $Z^1(W_F,\widehat{G})$ containing $\varphi$ is computed explicitly as a quotient of $\widehat{G} \times T \times \mu$ — the geometric description of the spectral side.

thm:2.2.1.summary: $L$-parameter side: when $Z(\widehat{G})$ is finite, $X_\varphi/\widehat{G} \cong [*/S_\psi] \times \mu$. The component of the moduli of $L$-parameters attached to a depth-zero supercuspidal packet is a point-mod-$S_\varphi$ times a discrete $\mu$-factor.

thm:3.1.2.summary: Representation side (Main Theorem): for $q$ above the Coxeter number, compact induction $\mathrm{c\text{-}Ind}_{G_x}^{G(F)}$ induces an equivalence $\mathcal{B}_{x,1} \to \mathcal{C}_{x,1}$ between the parahoric block and the depth-zero supercuspidal block of $G(F)$.

thm:3.1.3.summary: Every representation in the block $\mathcal{B}_{x,1}$ of a representation with regular supercuspidal reduction itself has supercuspidal reduction — the closure property making the block well-behaved.

thm:3.1.4.summary: Dichotomy for two vertices $x,y$: parahoric representations trivial on the pro-unipotent radical, one with supercuspidal reduction, are either linked in a controlled way or orthogonal — controls how blocks at different vertices interact.

thm:3.4.1.summary: $\Pi_{x,1} = \mathrm{c\text{-}Ind}_{G_x}^{G(F)} \sigma_{x,1}$ is a projective generator of the block $\mathcal{C}_{x,1}$ — the Morita-theoretic engine that turns the block into modules over its endomorphism algebra.

prop:3.2.2.summary: Deligne–Lusztig duality: $\mathbb{G}^F$-classes of pairs $(\mathbb{T},\theta)$ correspond to $\mathbb{G}^{*F^*}$-classes of semisimple pairs $(\mathbb{T}^*,s)$ — the dual-group dictionary for the finite group of Lie type $G_x$.

thm:3.2.4.summary: Broué's equivalence (imported): for $X_{\mathbb{U}}$ affine of dimension $d$ ($q$ above the Coxeter number), the cohomology $R\Gamma_c(X_{\mathbb{U}})$ of the Deligne–Lusztig variety induces a derived equivalence between blocks — the finite-group-of-Lie-type input.

thm:3.2.10.summary: Over $\overline{\mathbb{Z}}_\ell$-coefficients, members of the block of a regular-supercuspidal-reduction representation again have supercuspidal reduction — the integral refinement underpinning the block equivalence.

lem:5.1.1.summary: Nilpotent-singular-support reduction: $\mathcal{D}^b_{\mathrm{Coh},\mathrm{Nilp}}(C_\varphi) \cong \mathcal{D}^b_{\mathrm{Coh},\{0\}}(C_\varphi)$ — for this component the nilpotent singular-support condition collapses to support at the origin, simplifying the spectral side to perfect complexes.

prop:5.1.2.summary: The Fargues–Scholze spectral action by the line bundle $\mathcal{L}_\chi$ sends $\mathcal{D}(G_b(F))$ into $\mathcal{D}(G_{b-\chi}(F))$, i.e. it acts on the cohomology of $\mathrm{Bun}_G$ by translating Harder–Narasimhan strata — the mechanism linking the spectral and automorphic sides.

lem:5.2.1.summary: The Whittaker representation $W_{\vartheta,[\pi]}$ restricts to a finitely generated projective generator of $\mathrm{Rep}_\Lambda(G(F))_{[\pi]}$ — the object on which the spectral action is evaluated to build the equivalence functor.

prop:5.2.3.summary: Categorical equivalence: the functor $\Theta_0: \mathrm{Perf}(\mathbb{G}_m \times \mu_{\ell^k}) \to \mathcal{D}(\mathrm{Rep}_{\overline{\mathbb{Z}}_\ell}(G(F))_{[\pi]})^\omega$, $A \mapsto A \ast W_{\vartheta,[\pi]}$ (spectral action on the Whittaker generator), is an equivalence of derived categories — verifying the categorical local Langlands conjecture for depth-zero regular supercuspidals.

lem:5.2.5.summary: Generator criterion: a triangulated functor $F$ commuting with sums that sends a generator $E$ to a generator and induces isomorphisms $\mathrm{Hom}(E,E[n]) \cong \mathrm{Hom}(F(E),F(E[n]))$ for all $n$ is an equivalence — the abstract tool upgrading degree-zero faithfulness to the full equivalence.

edges.note: The quotient $X_\varphi/\widehat{G} \cong [*/S_\psi] \times \mu$ is read off the explicit description of the component $X_\varphi$.
The compact-induction equivalence is a Morita equivalence via the projective generator $\Pi_{x,1}$.
That $\Pi_{x,1}$ generates $\mathcal{C}_{x,1}$ uses that the block is closed under supercuspidal reduction.
Controlling generation across vertices uses the linkage/orthogonality dichotomy for parahoric blocks.
The supercuspidal-reduction closure is the integral ($\overline{\mathbb{Z}}_\ell$) block statement.
The block structure for the finite group of Lie type is obtained from Broué's Deligne–Lusztig equivalence.
Indexing the blocks uses the $(\mathbb{T},\theta) \leftrightarrow (\mathbb{T}^*,s)$ Deligne–Lusztig duality.
The source $\mathrm{Perf}(\mathbb{G}_m \times \mu_{\ell^k})$ is identified with perfect complexes on the $L$-parameter component $X_\varphi/\widehat{G}$ computed in Thm 2.2.1.
The target block $\mathrm{Rep}_\Lambda(G(F))_{[\pi]}$ is identified through the compact-induction equivalence.
The functor is defined by acting on the Whittaker projective generator $W_{\vartheta,[\pi]}$.
The functor is the spectral action, whose stratum-shifting behaviour (Prop 5.1.2) makes it land in the correct block.
The nilpotent-singular-support reduction collapses the spectral side to $\mathrm{Perf}$, the source of $\Theta_0$.
Full equivalence is concluded from degree-zero faithfulness via the generator criterion.

lparameter_side.description: Explicit computation of the component of the stack of $L$-parameters attached to a depth-zero TRSELP.

representation_side.description: Identification of the depth-zero supercuspidal block via compact induction and finite-group-of-Lie-type block theory.

categorical_equivalence.description: Matching the two sides through the Fargues–Scholze spectral action on the Whittaker generator.

FS.title: Geometrization of the local Langlands correspondence
FS.role: supplies the spectral action of $\mathrm{Perf}(Z^1(W_F,\widehat{G})/\widehat{G})$ on $D(\mathrm{Bun}_G)$ (used in Prop 5.1.2 and as the functor in Prop 5.2.3); the categorical conjecture verified here is the categorical form of the FS correspondence

broue1990isometries.title: Broué, Isométries parfaites, types de blocs, catégories dérivées
broue1990isometries.role: the perfect-isometry / derived block equivalence for Deligne–Lusztig varieties (Thm 3.2.4)

DeligneLusztig.title: Deligne–Lusztig, Representations of reductive groups over finite fields

DHKM.title: Dat–Helm–Kurinczuk–Moss, moduli of Langlands parameters
DHKM.role: the integral moduli space $Z^1(W_F,\widehat{G})$ whose components are computed in Chapter 2
